channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines from trusted sources

item.title: US launches fresh strikes on multiple targets in Iran as conflict escalates
item.description: The US military began a new round of strikes against multiple targets in Iran, hours after President Trump vowed further attacks if no peace deal is reached. The escalation comes amid ongoing tensions over Iran's nuclear program and attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: Ukraine strikes Russian-occupied Mariupol port, disrupting Crimean supply lines
item.description: Ukrainian forces hit the port of Mariupol in a drone attack, Kyiv said, the latest in a series of strikes targeting logistics connecting Russia to Crimea. The attack follows reports of fuel shortages in Crimea due to Ukrainian drone operations. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: US human rights officials meet North Korea detainee families in Seoul
item.description: Senior US human rights officials visiting South Korea met with families of South Koreans detained or abducted by North Korea, amid ongoing concerns about Pyongyang's human rights record and nuclear program. (Source: UPI)

item.title: Elon Musk accused of inflaming tensions in Belfast after knife attack sparks violence
item.description: The tech billionaire shared lists of protest locations on social media and blamed "uncontrolled immigration" for the knife attack in Belfast, which triggered anti-immigrant violence. The victim's family appealed for calm amid ongoing disorder. (Source: South China Morning Post)

item.title: UN to send investigators to Lebanon to assess potential war law violations
item.description: The UN human rights office will deploy a team of investigators to Lebanon next week to assess potential violations of international law by all parties during the ongoing conflict. The move comes amid continued Israeli strikes on Hezbollah targets. (Source: Al Arabiya English)

item.title: Chinese propagandists stoking dissent over Trump tariffs and data centres, OpenAI says
item.description: OpenAI reports that Chinese-speaking users were asked to use ChatGPT to create slogans and cartoons criticizing the US president's trade policies, highlighting ongoing influence operations. (Source: SCMP)

item.title: Pentagon's expanded blacklist of Chinese firms tests Xi's US visit
item.description: Washington's latest move highlights intense competition between the US and China, testing the fragile "stability" reached at the leadership summit in Beijing. (Source: SCMP)

item.title: Iran declares closure of Hormuz, threatens to attack ships in strait
item.description: Iran announced the closure of the strategic Strait of Hormuz after latest US strikes, threatening to attack any ships passing through the critical waterway. (Source: Times of Israel)

item.title: Massive new US strikes rock multiple targets inside Iran
item.description: Iranian state media networks confirm huge explosions across Bandar Abbas, Sirik and the nearby city of Minab in the southern part of the country amid a new wave of US attacks. (Source: TRT World)

item.title: Explosions and airstrikes reported across southern Iran amid new US attacks
item.description: Explosions and airstrikes were reported across southern Iran early Thursday amid a new wave of US attacks, according to local reports. (Source: Anadolu Agency)

item.title: Ukraine police chief: Russia recruits young women to kill Ukrainian servicemen
item.description: Ukraines police chief accused Russia of recruiting teenage Ukrainian girls to assassinate military personnel, following the arrest of a 17-year-old suspected of murdering a serviceman on instructions from a Russian operative. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: China-North Korea claim wins from Xi visit, but limits remain
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinpings visit to North Korea elevated Kim Jong Uns global stature and pulled Pyongyang closer into Beijings orbit, though analysts note significant limits remain in the bilateral relationship. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: Trump sticks with Pulte for intel job as risk grows of lapse in spy powers
item.description: A lapse in US foreign intelligence-gathering powers has grown more likely as President Trump continues to back his nominee for the intelligence post amid congressional gridlock. (Source: AP News)

item.title: Israeli Airstrikes Kill 13 in Southern Lebanon as Hezbollah Conflict Escalates
item.description: Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon killed at least 13 people on Wednesday, Lebanese security sources said, as Israel pressed its campaign against Hezbollah, with the Iran-backed group claiming fresh attacks against Israeli forces in the south. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: US Inflation Hits Three-Year High at 4.2% as Iran War Drives Energy Prices Higher
item.description: Consumer prices rose at the fastest pace in three years in May, reaching 4.2% annually, as the Middle East conflict sent energy costs surging and gave the Federal Reserve ammunition to keep interest rates unchanged into 2027. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: UN Nuclear Watchdog Demands Iran Report Enriched Uranium Stocks
item.description: The IAEA's 35-nation Board of Governors passed a US-backed resolution telling Iran to declare its remaining enriched uranium stocks and allow inspectors to verify them, a move that could complicate Washington's ongoing talks with Tehran. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: Erdogan warns Israel's attacks on Syria, Lebanon now threaten Turkey
item.description: Turkish President Erdogan stated that Israel's military operations in Syria and Lebanon have escalated to the point where they threaten Turkey itself, calling Israel's aggression a threat to the whole world. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: Ukraine partners with Taurus missile maker for deep strike systems
item.description: Ukrainian defense company Ukrainian Armor and European firm MBDA initiated a strategic partnership to develop deep strike capabilities, enhancing Ukraine's indigenous defense production. (Source: Kyiv Independent)

item.title: Countries vie to become energy safe haven as Hormuz crisis deepens
item.description: Multiple nations are pitching the Trump administration on energy alternatives as the Strait of Hormuz remains disrupted, seeking to position themselves as secure energy suppliers. (Source: Politico)

item.title: Ireland sets online safety, security priorities for EU presidency
item.description: Ireland's EU presidency will focus on improving online safety for children and boosting Europe's economic competitiveness and security amid growing geopolitical challenges. (Source: Irish Times)

item.title: Bank of Japan Governor Ueda Hospitalised, Will Miss Key Policy Meeting
item.description: BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda has been hospitalised for medical treatment and will miss the June 15-16 policy meeting, the central bank announced, raising uncertainty about Japans monetary policy direction during a critical period. (Source: Reuters)

item.title: Israel Kills 16 in Lebanon as UN Announces International Law Probe
item.description: Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon killed at least 16 people as UN investigators announced they will arrive next week to assess potential violations of international law by all parties in the escalating conflict. (Source: Al Jazeera)

item.title: Trump Says He Loves Inflation as Prices Hit 3-Year High
item.description: President Donald Trump dismissed concerns about surging consumer prices, saying I love the inflation and claiming the U.S. is taking millions of barrels of Iranian oil, as inflation reached its highest level in three years. (Source: AP News)

item.title: Violent Unrest Erupts in Belfast Over Knife Attack Arrest
item.description: Police in Northern Ireland used water cannons against protesters as violent unrest flared in Belfast following the arrest of a Sudanese man charged with attempted murder in a stabbing attack, with officials calling for calm. (Source: AP News)

item.title: NATO Forward Land Forces Stand Up New Multinational Battlegroup in Finland
item.description: NATO has stood up its Forward Land Forces Finland during a ceremony at the Swedish Army base, establishing the alliances newest multinational battlegroup as part of its enhanced deterrence posture in Northern Europe. (Source: Janes)
